evals.name: standalone-creation
evals.expected_output: Detects no upstream ROADMAP or PRD and no existing BRIEF. Validates the topic slug 'checkout-flow' matches [a-z0-9-]+ at Phase 0. Starts Phase 1 (discover) to ground the feature's problem and outcome conversationally. Produces a BRIEF doc with all five required sections at docs/briefs/BRIEF-checkout-flow.md. Phase 4 spawns two reviewer agents in parallel (content quality, structural format).
expectations: Plan validates the topic slug 'checkout-flow' matches [a-z0-9-]+ at Phase 0
Plan starts from Phase 0 (setup) or Phase 1 (discover) since no BRIEF artifact exists
Plan includes a Phase 1 (discover) scoping step anchored on the feature's problem/outcome pair before drafting
Plan includes Phase 2 (draft) producing the Problem Statement and User Outcome sections
Plan includes Phase 3 (structural fill) producing the User Journeys and Scope Boundary sections
Plan includes Phase 4 (validate) with exactly two parallel reviewer agents (content quality, structural format)
Plan does NOT include an altitude reviewer at Phase 4
Plan includes Phase 5 (finalize) with explicit human approval before status transition
Plan mentions reading phase reference files (e.g. references/phases/phase-0-setup.md)
Plan mentions detecting repo visibility from CLAUDE.md or repo path
Plan produces the BRIEF at docs/briefs/BRIEF-checkout-flow.md

evals.name: upstream-roadmap-grounding
evals.expected_output: Detects the ROADMAP path as upstream input. Canonicalizes the path at Phase 0 and confirms it resolves inside the repo working tree. Reads the ROADMAP content to derive the feature's problem/outcome candidate during Phase 1. Carries forward the upstream framing into the BRIEF's Problem Statement. Declares the ROADMAP path as the BRIEF's frontmatter `upstream`.
expectations: Plan detects the ROADMAP path argument as upstream input
Plan canonicalizes the upstream path at Phase 0 and bounds-checks it against the repo working tree
Plan reads the ROADMAP document content during Phase 1 (discover) to derive the feature's problem and outcome
Plan carries forward upstream ROADMAP framing into the Problem Statement section
Plan declares the ROADMAP path as the BRIEF frontmatter upstream field

evals.name: artifact-decision-from-rich-issue
evals.prompt: /brief A PRD-ready feature request: the search box should debounce input and the team already wrote a detailed problem and outcome in the issue body.
evals.expected_output: Phase 0 surfaces the produce-vs-hand-off artifact decision because the chain is entered partway up — the supplied evidence already implies both the feature's problem and its outcome. The skill weighs whether to produce a durable brief or hand the existing evidence forward to the PRD, rather than writing a brief by reflex. It records the decision (produce or handed-off) before proceeding.
expectations: Plan recognizes that the chain is being entered partway up (a rich issue body already implies problem and outcome)
Plan surfaces the produce-vs-hand-off artifact decision at Phase 0
Plan weighs whether to produce a durable brief or hand the existing evidence forward to the PRD
Plan notes that writing a brief by reflex is not the goal
Plan records the artifact decision (produce or handed-off) before proceeding
Plan describes the hand-off path exiting the workflow without producing a brief when the framing already exists

evals.name: jury-two-reviewers
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 spawns exactly two reviewer agents in parallel via the Agent tool with run_in_background: true: content quality and structural format. No altitude reviewer. Each reviewer prompt opens with a fixed preamble framing the BRIEF as data-under-review. Each prompt pins the verdict file path. Each prompt requires a literal **Verdict:** PASS | FAIL marker. The orchestrator aggregates by an all-PASS rule before Phase 5.
expectations: Plan describes Phase 4 spawning exactly two reviewer agents in parallel
Plan names the two distinct reviewer roles (content quality, structural format)
Plan explicitly states there is no altitude reviewer (a brief frames one feature, so there is no altitude band to police)
Plan mentions the Agent tool with run_in_background: true for jury parallelism
Plan mentions each reviewer prompt opens with a fixed preamble framing BRIEF content as data-under-review
Plan mentions each verdict file path is pinned (subagent does not choose its output location)
Plan mentions verdicts use a structured **Verdict:** PASS | FAIL marker
Plan aggregates verdicts by an all-PASS rule before proceeding to Phase 5

evals.name: missing-required-section-rejection
evals.expected_output: The structural-format reviewer at Phase 4 (and shirabe validate, FC04) requires all five required sections — Status, Problem Statement, User Outcome, User Journeys, Scope Boundary — present and ordered. A draft missing any required section fails the structural reviewer and FC04, and the verdict names the missing section. The jury does not return all-PASS, so the brief cannot reach Phase 5.
expectations: Plan requires all five required sections (Status, Problem Statement, User Outcome, User Journeys, Scope Boundary) present and in order
Plan notes the structural-format reviewer checks required-section presence at Phase 4
Plan notes shirabe validate FC04 rejects a brief missing a required section and names the missing section
Plan describes a missing-section draft failing the jury (not all-PASS) so it cannot reach Phase 5

evals.name: invalid-status-rejection
evals.expected_output: The frontmatter status must be one of Draft, Accepted, Done (FC02). The structural-format reviewer and shirabe validate reject a status value outside that set (for example Published) and the error lists the valid statuses. The body ## Status first non-blank line must be the bare status word matching the frontmatter status (FC03).
expectations: Plan requires the frontmatter status to be one of Draft, Accepted, Done (FC02)
Plan notes shirabe validate FC02 rejects a status value outside the valid set and lists the valid statuses
Plan notes the body ## Status first non-blank line must be the bare status word matching the frontmatter status (FC03)
Plan notes prose on the ## Status first line breaks FC03

evals.name: lifecycle-accept-verb
evals.expected_output: Detects 'accept' as a lifecycle verb rather than starting the creation workflow. Validates the Open Questions section is empty or removed before transition. Runs skills/brief/scripts/transition-status.sh with target Accepted. The file stays in docs/briefs/ (no directory movement for any brief transition).
expectations: Plan detects 'accept' as a lifecycle verb rather than starting the creation workflow
Plan validates the Open Questions section is empty or removed before transition
Plan invokes skills/brief/scripts/transition-status.sh to perform the transition
Plan targets transitioning from Draft to Accepted status
Plan does NOT move the file to a different directory for the Accepted transition
Plan notes there is no reason argument for the accept transition

evals.name: topic-slug-validation
evals.expected_output: Phase 0 rejects the topic slug because it does not match [a-z0-9-]+. The skill stops before drafting and reports the constraint to the user. No BRIEF document is created.
expectations: Plan validates the topic slug against the [a-z0-9-]+ constraint at Phase 0
Plan rejects the input because the slug contains forbidden characters (., /)
Plan stops before drafting begins
Plan does NOT create a BRIEF document
Plan explains the slug constraint to the user
